question_text: A gas has a pressure of 80 kPa when its volume is 500 cm³. If the volume is reduced to 250 cm³ at constant temperature, what is the new pressure?
correct_answer: 160 kPa. Using Boyle's Law (P₁V₁ = P₂V₂): 80 × 500 = P₂ × 250, so P₂ = 40,000/250 = 160 kPa
explanation: When temperature is constant, pressure and volume are inversely proportional. Halving the volume doubles the pressure because the same number of gas particles now occupy half the space.
